topic: Membach SG null (1999 European total solar eclipse) — verdict reframe to Refuted by Data via the κ Coupling Cluster vise (EXP-301)
our_argument_summary: WIN-013 was just reframed Misleading → Refuted by Data via EXP-301 (κ Coupling Cluster). The verdict change rests on a tight, structural argument: the dome publishes κ = 1.677 nT/µGal as a constant coupling (WIN-012, predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json kappa_coupling_formula = 'Δg = ΔB/1.672', monitor.py line 477 'KAPPA = 1.67' / Domain 7 in source numbering); applying it to the documented 1999 European-eclipse ΔB (Strestík 2001 ΔY≈+10 ΔX≈−5 nT verified DOI 10.1007/978-94-010-0800-6_55; Curto 2006 multi-site model verified DOI 10.1029/2005JA011499) implies a contact-phase Δg of ~3–6 µGal at Membach; the SG continuously sampled at 0.01–0.1 µGal precision and recorded zero. The vise is symmetric: any carve-out that exempts 1999 from κ also destroys PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal August 2026 derivation, because κ is the only published derivation route to that target (verified in predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3). The structural argument and the math are correct. Two of the four anchor citations the prose leans on, however, do not survive a fresh fact-check.

relevant_files: raw-text/01-home.txt (κ = 1.67 nT/uGal published as model parameter)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (Domain 5 in dashboard numbering = EM-Gravity Coupling κ)
github.com/john09289/predictions/main/monitor.py (line 477 '# ── 7. EM-Gravity Coupling κ ──', KAPPA=1.67, kappa_obs=10.9/6.5, no Membach domain)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py Domain 7 (EM-Gravity Coupling κ): hardcoded KAPPA = 1.67 (line 214), then computes kappa_obs = 10.9 / 6.5 ≈ 1.677, computes err_kappa = |KAPPA - kappa_obs| / kappa_obs * 100, marks pass if err < 5%. Both numerator (10.9) and denominator (6.5) are static literals, so the 'pass' status is a tautology — the static derivation cannot do anything else. There is no domain in monitor.py for Membach 1999 / Van Camp / SG-null verification. The dome's automated 'check' for κ does not touch WIN-013's claimed observation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: monitor.py for κ is fully hardcoded (KAPPA, 10.9, 6.5 all static literals). No Membach SG / Van Camp data fetch. WIN-013's 0.0 µGal value is editorial in 02-wins.txt, never enters monitor.py.
data_sources_used: BOU 2017 magnetometer (-10.9 nT) — referenced via WIN-010 anchor, hardcoded in monitor.py KAPPA computation
Mohe 1997 spring gravimeter (6.5 µGal) — hardcoded in monitor.py kappa_obs computation; quality contested (WIN-011)
Membach 1999 SG (0.0 µGal) — referenced editorially in dome's wins.txt, not in monitor.py
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: monitor.py Domain 7 has 5% tolerance and is hardcoded — no escape needed. The escape applies to PRED-TIER-3, where the monitoring layer (Domain 10/11) gates eclipse-prediction tests on Kp<2 preconditions; a high-Kp August 2026 day silently skips the κ test rather than recording a fail. This is the broader monitor.py escape pattern, not specific to WIN-013.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our review's 'monitor.py Domain #7' for κ matches monitor.py's source-code comment numbering (line 477: '# ── 7. EM-Gravity Coupling κ ──'). However, the dome's PUBLIC live-power dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt) renumbers κ as 'Domain 5' (Domain 7 in the dashboard is GPS Sagnac Verification). A reader checking the dome's dashboard for verification will hit the wrong domain and may conclude our citation is wrong. The factual claim is correct (monitor.py source numbers κ at 7), but the verification path is non-obvious and creates a fact-check trap.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no Membach domain, no Van Camp data fetch, no SG-null verification logic. The 0.0 µGal figure attributed to 'Van Camp 1999' lives only as editorial text in raw-text/02-wins.txt. monitor.py Domain 7 is the κ definition (KAPPA=1.67, kappa_obs=10.9/6.5), tautologically validating the coupling constant against itself; it does not check whether the SG null at Membach is consistent with κ-implied predictions.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics has no expectation of an eclipse-induced SG signal at Membach in 1999 (no peer-reviewed mechanism would predict a few-µGal contact-phase pulse from a partial-totality eclipse pass-by at that latitude). The dome model is making a non-standard prediction (via κ), and the SG null refutes the dome prediction. There is no standard-physics explanation being relabeled here — the dome is making its own prediction and that prediction failed.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Van Camp's underlying Membach SG observation was made in August 1999 and the dome model V51 was first published in 2024 (per dome's own version metadata in 04-coordinates.txt and 06-evolution.txt). The 0.0 µGal result was publicly indexed for ~25 years before the dome cited it as confirming the model. The dome's monitor.py contains no entry for Membach / 1999 / Van Camp, so even the weaker commit-timestamp 'genuinely-prospective' test is inapplicable — there is no commit-pinned prediction to compare against.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 0.0 µGal value is not derived from dome geometry — it is the observational reading the dome adopts and labels confirming. The dome's only derivation chain involving κ runs from BOU 2017 ΔB and Mohe 1997 Δg (also adopted observations) through the ratio 10.9/6.5; nothing in that chain starts from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any other dome-geometric parameter.

holes_found.description: Headline citation 'Van Camp et al. 2001 (Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.) DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637' does not resolve. doi.org returns HTTP 404. Crossref API returns 'Resource not found' for the DOI. Crossref title-search for Van Camp + Membach + eclipse + 1999/2001 in any journal returns no matching paper (closest results: Van Camp 2004 Ardenne uplift in GJI; Van Camp 2006 hydrogeology in JGR; Van Camp 2007 rainfall in Journal of Geodesy; Francis/Van Camp 1997 calibration chapter in IAG Symposia — none are the eclipse SG-null paper). Worse, the DOI prefix 10.1023 is Springer/Kluwer, but PEPI is published by Elsevier (DOI prefix 10.1016) — so the cited DOI prefix is internally inconsistent with the cited journal. Prior cycle reviews (c3 citations_verified, c4 citations_verified) marked this DOI 'verified' on the false basis that '10.1023 = Kluwer/Springer = consistent with PEPI publication'; that prefix logic is wrong (PEPI ≠ Kluwer/Springer). This is the headline empirical anchor of the just-integrated 'Refuted by Data' verdict change. A defender clicking the DOI and finding 404 will use this to attack the entire reframe.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the Van Camp 2001 PEPI DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 with a verifiable source for the Membach SG 1999 eclipse null. Concrete options: (a) Cite the Royal Observatory of Belgium / Geophysical Centre Membach SG public time-series archive directly (continuous 1995-present, 1-second sampling, available via http://www.seismologie.be/ or Eurasian SG network archive); (b) Use Van Camp's verified peer-reviewed Membach papers as supporting context — DOI 10.1111/j.1365-246x.2004.02310.x (GJI 2004, Ardenne uplift via long-term Membach gravity, confirms continuous SG operation 1995+) and DOI 10.1029/2006jb004405 (JGR 2006, hydrogeology corrections at Membach, confirms 0.01–0.1 µGal noise-floor performance) — these don't directly assert the 1999 eclipse null but jointly establish that a continuously-operating low-noise SG was at Membach when the eclipse passed; (c) cite the dome's own attribution ('the dome cites "Van Camp 1999, 0.0 µGal" as confirming the model') without claiming a specific peer-reviewed paper; (d) flag the citation as '[citation needed — DOI does not resolve as of 2026-05-10]' pending operator follow-up. Option (a) + (b) together is the cleanest fix: the SG and its noise floor are independently verifiable; the eclipse null at that station follows from continuous operation.

holes_found.description: WIN-014 (paired with this WIN via EXP-301; same κ-cluster vise) cites 'Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 (Adv Space Res)' with link https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0273117718300656 to support the 5–15 nT order-of-magnitude framing for the 2009 East Asian eclipse magnetic perturbations. That PII resolves to a different paper (Park, Matsuzawa, Inamori — 'Nanosatellite constellation deployment using on-board magnetic torquer interaction with space plasma' — verified via Crossref API for 10.1016/j.asr.2018.X). Crossref searches by author 'Tomás Mendoza-Torres' + 'eclipse' return no matching 2018 Adv. Space Res. paper. The citation appears to be either the wrong PII for a real paper or a non-existent reference. While this hole is on WIN-014's prose, the κ-cluster section (6.14) — also integrated by EXP-301 — uses the same Tomás 2018 ScienceDirect link to support the 2009 ΔB framing. Same critical citation problem on the section side. (This is in the WIN-013 review because (a) c5 cycle for WIN-014 will mirror this finding and (b) the κ-cluster section uses the same cite; flag now so decider can fix all three loci together.)
holes_found.recommendation: Replace Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 with verified citations. The dome's own WIN-068 39-eclipse 1991-2016 ensemble (207 magnetic measurements) IS sufficient to establish the 5–15 nT order-of-magnitude — it's already in the dome's own published material and our review treats it (in WIN-068) as Std Model Explains. Cite WIN-068 directly as our ensemble-magnitude source. For the 2009 East Asian eclipse specifically, cite verified primary sources: DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2012.07.005 ('Response of low latitude D-region ionosphere to the total solar eclipse of 22 July 2009') or Yamazaki & Maute 2017 review (10.1007/s11214-016-0287-7) for the 2009 eclipse magnetic context. Same fix applies to section 6.14 (κ Coupling Cluster) prose.

holes_found.description: 'monitor.py Domain #7' is the citation we use for κ in WIN-013 detail_evidence (verified accurate against monitor.py line 477 source comment '# ── 7. EM-Gravity Coupling κ ──'); but the dome's PUBLIC live-power dashboard renders κ as 'Domain 5' in its tile listing (with Domain 7 in the dashboard listing being 'GPS Sagnac Verification'). A reader who clicks through to the dome's live-power page to 'verify Domain #7 = κ' will find Domain 7 = GPS Sagnac and may conclude our review made an error. The factual claim is correct against the source-code numbering; the verification path through the dashboard is misleading. This same Domain-#7 phrasing appears in WIN-014 detail_evidence and in the κ-cluster section 6.14 (subsection 'What κ is and why the dome cannot abandon it').
holes_found.recommendation: Reword to 'monitor.py code section 7 (the EM-Gravity Coupling κ block at line 477 of monitor.py; renumbered as Domain 5 in the dome's live-power dashboard tiles)' OR drop the numeric anchor entirely and cite κ by name: 'monitor.py KAPPA constant and EM-Gravity Coupling block; predictions JSON kappa_coupling_formula FORM-V51-004'. The latter is cleaner and avoids the verification trap. Apply same change in WIN-014, WIN-058b, and section 6.14 wherever 'Domain #7' appears as the κ anchor.

holes_found.description: 'Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002' citation (with link https://www.researchgate.net/publication/29623808_Solar_eclipse_effect_on_geomagnetic_induction_parameters) is unverified. Crossref searches by author and title return no matching peer-reviewed paper. ResearchGate links are not authoritative DOIs (the link could lead to a preprint, conference paper, or moved/missing record). Lower severity than holes 1-2 because the magnetic perturbation magnitude is independently corroborated by Strestík 2001 (verified) and Curto 2006 (verified), which together cover the 5–15 nT range without needing Hvoždara. But a citation we link should resolve.
holes_found.recommendation: Either verify the Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002 publication via a DOI lookup (it may be a Contributions to Geophysics & Geodesy paper at a real DOI) or remove the citation and rely on Strestík + Curto, which already span the same magnitude range. If we keep it, cite it as 'Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002, Contributions to Geophysics and Geodesy 32 (or whatever the actual venue is), no DOI located' — making the verification status explicit.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence understates the magnetic perturbation upper bound: tldr says '~5–10 nT at central European observatories,' but detail_evidence cites '5–15 nT' (Hvoždara reports up to 12–15 nT for ΔY; Strestík reports +10 nT for ΔY). The tldr's upper bound of 10 nT is below the cited detail's upper bound of 15 nT. A defender could nitpick that we hide the wider range in the TLDR to make our predicted Δg range (3–6 µGal) look tighter than the detail allows (3–9 µGal). Acceptable simplification but improves rigor to widen.
holes_found.recommendation: Change tldr_evidence to '~5–15 nT at central European observatories' (or '~5–10 nT for the ΔX-only band') so the TLDR matches the detail's range exactly. Same patch applies symmetrically to WIN-014 tldr_evidence which uses '5–15 nT' (already correct there).

holes_found.description: WIN-013 detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text use κ = 1.677 (computed as 10.9/6.5 = 1.6769). The dome's own published materials give κ at three different precisions: monitor.py KAPPA = 1.67 (line 214), predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json kappa_coupling_formula 'Δg = ΔB / 1.672' (formula_id FORM-V51-004), and live-power dashboard uses 1.67 to 1.677. Our 1.677 is the highest-precision computed value but a defender can argue 'you're using a value the dome doesn't publish exactly' — since the dome publishes 1.67/1.672 in different official files. The Δg computation is unaffected at 2 sig figs (5/1.677 = 2.98 vs 5/1.672 = 2.99 µGal), but rhetorical hygiene improves if we acknowledge the dome's own κ publishing inconsistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a one-sentence parenthetical to detail_evidence: 'The dome's own published materials give κ at three precisions (1.67 in monitor.py, 1.672 in predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json, 1.677 in live-power.txt); we use 1.677 as the highest-precision computed value, but the predicted-Δg ranges below are robust to either choice at 2 significant figures.' Alternatively swap 1.677 → 1.672 (the value the dome's own forward-prediction JSON uses) and note the equivalence.

holes_found.description: Cross-reference 'See WIN-011 for the Mohe spring-gravimeter quality argument and WIN-012 for the κ-definition circularity' is correct as written (verified WIN-011 verdict 'Not Demonstrated' and WIN-012 verdict 'Self-Contradicted' in current data). However, the κ-cluster section 6.14 makes the same cross-reference and additionally references WIN-058b's Monte Carlo finding ('Monte Carlo at physically realistic SNR produces r > 0.96 from shared forcing alone, as the WIN-058b detail already documents'). Verified that WIN-058b detail_evidence does contain this claim. No bug here, just confirming the cross-section dependency holds.
holes_found.recommendation: No change needed; this hole is included for tracker completeness — confirms the EXP-301 cross-references all resolve correctly.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json file CONFIRMS κ as a published divisor formula: 'kappa_coupling_formula: "Δg(μGal) = ΔB(nT) / 1.672"' (formula_id FORM-V51-004). This is a stronger primary source than monitor.py because (a) it's the dome's forward-prediction registration file (OTS-anchored per dome's methodology), and (b) it explicitly states the divisor form Δg = ΔB / κ that our review uses. The κ-cluster section's prose currently cites monitor.py and live-power; adding the predictions JSON FORM-V51-004 anchor strengthens the 'dome publishes κ as a constant' claim and makes any 'κ has scope conditions' defense fight uphill — the formula's structure has no scope condition encoded.

Crossley et al. 2013 / IAG2009 SG Workshop noise-floor claim '0.1–1 µGal at the relevant timescales' is good but Van Camp's own Membach noise-floor work (2004 Ardenne paper, 2006 hydrogeology paper) gives a tighter and Membach-specific number: residual noise after tide+pressure correction is documented at < 0.1 µGal at minute timescales. Citing Van Camp's own hydrogeology paper (DOI 10.1029/2006jb004405) for the Membach noise floor is doubly strong: it's a verified DOI, and it's the same author the dome credits for the eclipse null — making the noise-floor argument extremely hard to attack on 'wrong instrument-class' grounds.
sources: DOI 10.1029/2006jb004405 (Van Camp et al. 2006, JGR Solid Earth, hydrogeological corrections at Membach SG)
DOI 10.1111/j.1365-246x.2004.02310.x (Francis, Van Camp et al. 2004, GJI, long-term Membach gravity uplift)

stronger_arguments.description: The vise argument can be strengthened by noting the dome's PRED-TIER-3 itself does not encode any scope condition for κ — verified: predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3 entry has no 'kp_precondition', 'coverage_threshold', or 'bedrock_band' field, only the 10–13 µGal target. So the 'κ has scope conditions' defense, if mounted, requires the dome to retroactively annotate PRED-TIER-3 with conditions absent from the registered prediction — exactly the post-hoc rescue the OTS canary identifies as the dome's signature methodological vulnerability. Tying this directly to the canary tab is high-value cross-linking.
sources: docs/index.html — Timestamp Error tab (for the OTS canary cross-link)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome is right that the Membach SG measured zero during the 1999 European eclipse; the SG was operating, the eclipse passed over central Europe at high coverage, and a low-noise null is not contested by anyone (data/wins.json:013 finding cites the dome's own attribution of '0.0 µGal'). The dome is also right that, naively, an electromagnetic-only effect would be expected to produce no gravimetric signature — the SG null IS consistent with 'no gravity coupling.' Within a model that makes no other claim about EM-gravity coupling, the dome's interpretation 'confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational' is internally consistent (raw-text/02-wins.txt). This is the kernel: a real null at a real instrument that, in isolation, is unremarkable.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: What makes the κ-cluster vise force the 'Refuted by Data' verdict is that the dome does NOT in fact restrict itself to 'no EM-gravity coupling.' It explicitly publishes a coupling, κ = 1.677 (per data/wins.json:012; predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json:kappa_coupling_formula FORM-V51-004; raw-text/05-model.txt 'coupling = 10.9 / 6.5 = 1.67 nT/uGal'), and uses it to derive PRED-TIER-3's headline 10–13 µGal August 2026 forecast. Once κ is published as a constant, every past eclipse with documented ΔB and a co-located operating SG is a κ-prediction-test case. The 1999 Membach configuration is exactly one of those — and the κ-implied prediction (3–6 µGal at C1/C4) is 30–600× the SG noise floor (raw-text/08-live-power.txt, predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3 implies same instrument class as 2026). The dome's interpretation only saves the claim if κ has scope conditions exempting 1999 — which would also exempt 2026, killing PRED-TIER-3.

deeper_analysis:
The verdict reframe to Refuted by Data (Misleading → Refuted by Data) is structurally defensible — the κ-cluster vise is the right argument and the math (Δg = ΔB/κ → 3–6 µGal vs SG noise floor 0.01–0.1 µGal → 30–600× margin) checks out. The structural argument survives all of the citation issues flagged above; even if every external DOI vanished, the argument 'dome publishes κ, dome publishes Membach 0.0 µGal as confirming, the math then implies a measurement that did not occur' relies only on the dome's own published material (raw-text/02-wins.txt, raw-text/05-model.txt, predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json) — verified.

The exposure is purely on rhetorical/credibility grounds: a defender or third-party reviewer who fact-checks our headline DOI and finds 404 will leverage that to discredit the entire reframe (especially because the verdict change FROM Misleading (a methodological argument) TO Refuted by Data (an empirical argument) raises the citation-rigor bar). Prior cycles (c3, c4) verified the Van Camp DOI on incorrect prefix-matching logic (10.1023 = Springer; PEPI is Elsevier 10.1016 — these are inconsistent), so the verification process was already broken.

Counter-argument from the dome-defender side I considered: 'maybe the DOI is just orphaned because of the Kluwer→Springer migration, the paper exists.' Plausible, but Crossref's database covers Kluwer-era DOIs and explicitly returns 'Resource not found' for this one. Title-search for any Van Camp + Membach + eclipse + 1999 paper in any journal also returns null. If the paper exists, it's not findable through the standard fact-checking channels a reviewer would use. So even if the underlying empirical claim is true, the citation chain is broken in the way that matters for credibility. (Note: c4 review acknowledged the year-discrepancy issue — eclipse 1999 vs publication 2001 — but did not test the DOI itself. That's the audit trail.)

Why I rate the verdict 'still holds' despite two critical citation holes: the structural argument (vise) does not depend on those citations. Replace them with the dome's own attribution + Crossley 2013 SG noise floor + Strestík 2001 ΔB (all verifiable) and the verdict survives unchanged. The patch path is straightforward.

Newly considered angles in this cycle (advocate mode + cross-WIN consistency + quantitative verification):
(1) Advocate mode: the strongest dome rebuttal would be 'κ has scope conditions documented in [some forthcoming paper]; 1999/2009 fall outside them.' Pre-empted by: PRED-TIER-3 currently encodes no scope conditions (verified predictions.json), so any retroactive scope is post-hoc — same canary signature as the OTS error. Worth surfacing this pre-emption in the WIN prose, not just the section.
(2) Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-013 (this WIN), WIN-014 (paired EXP-301 reframe), WIN-058b (storm corollary), section 6.14 all share the κ anchor. If we change citations on WIN-013, decider must mirror on WIN-014 and section 6.14 — same patch motion. Track explicitly so we don't fix one and leave two stale.
(3) Quantitative verification: ran the math on the κ-divisor with 5–15 nT ΔB → 3–9 µGal Δg span (matches detail_evidence within rounding). SG noise floor 0.01–0.1 µGal at minute timescales (Membach-specific per Van Camp 2006 verified DOI) → margin 30–900×, conservative claim of 30–600× checks out. PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal target ÷ κ ≈ 17–22 nT predicted ΔB at the August 2026 eclipse path — exactly the magnitude of past eclipses, so PRED-TIER-3 is internally consistent with applying κ uniformly to past and future eclipses. That is what makes the vise tight: there is no asymmetry in the dome's own forward formula that would let past eclipses opt out.

citations_verified: Curto et al. 2006 (J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 111 A03306) DOI 10.1029/2005JA011499 — Crossref confirms title 'Modeling the geomagnetic effects caused by the solar eclipse of 11 August 1999' authors Curto/Heilig/Piñol; resolves correctly.

Strestík (Střeštík) 2001 chapter DOI 10.1007/978-94-010-0800-6_55 — Crossref confirms title 'The Response of the 11 August 1999 Total Solar Eclipse in the Geomagnetic Field' in book 'Earth-Moon Relationships' 2001; resolves correctly.

Yang & Wang 2002 (Astrophysics and Space Science) DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985 — Crossref confirms title 'Gravity Anomaly During the Mohe Total Solar Eclipse and New Constraint on Gravitational Shielding Parameter'; resolves correctly. Cited via WIN-011 cross-reference; the dome attributed Mohe to Wang et al. 2000 originally.

Crossley et al. 2013 link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 — referenced for SG residual noise floor 0.1–1 µGal; URL resolves (HTTP 303 redirect, expected for Springer DOI URLs); not formally DOI-checked beyond URL response but consistent with cited content.
monitor.py line 477 '# ── 7. EM-Gravity Coupling κ ──' (KAPPA=1.67, kappa_obs=10.9/6.5) — fetched directly from github.com/john09289/predictions/main/monitor.py; verified.
predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json kappa_coupling_formula = 'Δg = ΔB / 1.672' formula_id FORM-V51-004 — verified in the cloned repo.
data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3 entry — claim 'SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals'; test_window 'August 2026'; verified.
citations_failed: Van Camp et al. 2001 (Phys. Earth Planet. Inter.) DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 — doi.org returns HTTP 404 (verified 2026-05-10); Crossref API returns 'Resource not found' for the DOI; Crossref title-search returns no Van Camp + Membach + eclipse paper at this or any DOI; DOI prefix 10.1023 is Springer/Kluwer, inconsistent with PEPI which uses Elsevier 10.1016 prefix. CRITICAL — headline citation of the just-integrated reframe.

Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 (Adv. Space Res.) URL https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0273117718300656 — PII resolves to a different paper (Park et al., 'Nanosatellite constellation deployment using on-board magnetic torquer interaction with space plasma', verified via Crossref API); Crossref author+title search returns no matching Tomás/Mendoza-Torres eclipse-magnetic ensemble paper. CRITICAL — used in WIN-014 detail and section 6.14 prose.

Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002 ResearchGate URL https://www.researchgate.net/publication/29623808_Solar_eclipse_effect_on_geomagnetic_induction_parameters — Crossref author+title search returns no peer-reviewed match; ResearchGate URL is not an authoritative DOI. MODERATE — the 5–15 nT range is independently established by Strestík + Curto, so this cite is redundant if we drop or replace it.
citations_unchecked: doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 (Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013, dome's closest verified record for the 2009 East Asian SG null) — DOI returns HTTP 302 redirect (CJG uses non-doi.org resolver); not deep-verified this cycle but appears to follow CJG's documented DOI pattern. Flagged for next-cycle full verification.
Hampel et al. 2003 Membach vault EM specs — proposed in stronger_arguments since c2/c3, never written to prose, still unverified. Carry-forward.

summary_for_decider:
This is a c5 review of the just-integrated EXP-301 verdict reframe (Misleading → Refuted by Data) for WIN-013. The structural argument is correct and the verdict is defensible. Two CRITICAL citation holes block the reframe from being airtight: (1) Van Camp 2001 PEPI DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 does not resolve and the prefix is wrong for the journal; this is the headline empirical anchor; (2) Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 cite (used in WIN-014 and section 6.14 — this finding migrates) is unverifiable. Patches are scoped: replace headline DOI with verifiable alternatives (Van Camp 2006 hydrogeology DOI 10.1029/2006jb004405 + dome's own attribution); replace Tomás 2018 with WIN-068's own ensemble + DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2012.07.005. Plus a moderate Domain-#7 verification trap (monitor.py source numbers κ at 7, but the dome's public dashboard renumbers it 5 — confusing readers) and minor TLDR/κ-precision tightenings. Recommended action: minor_edit (4-5 patches), but escalate to operator-aware if citation replacements are non-obvious; the verdict change is operator-commissioned (HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-013-014-VERDICT-REFRAME-001) so the operator should be aware that an unverifiable headline DOI was just integrated and may want to weigh in on the replacement.

Cross-WIN propagation: hole 1 (Van Camp DOI) and hole 2 (Tomás DOI) appear in WIN-014 c5 and section 6.14 c1 too — same patches needed there. Don't fix WIN-013 in isolation. Fix all three loci together. The next priority-queue item (329, WIN-014, deep-attack) and item 330 (p6-614-kappa-cluster, section-new, deep-attack) will surface the same critical citations on review; the decider can pre-empt that by patching all three loci atomically based on this WIN-013 review.

Verdict: current_verdict_holds=true with confidence 0.78 (high; downgraded from 1.0 by the citation exposure). queue_id=328 set; matches queue item; queue_pushed_at=2026-05-10T14:15:29.394Z.
